refines.reason: The apparent [RBP+0x7F] local byte in wrapper 0x140153190 aliases incoming stack arg 5, and the only direct caller passes SIL. That SIL is set by SETZ after comparing an interpreter slot field [RBX+0x104] with 1.
The 0x140153C00 wrapper receives R9B from the same SETZ-style parent family and later propagates it through SIL into the 0x140154470 request-prep call.

resolved_sources.prologue_alias: 0x14015319F PUSH RBP; 0x1401531A0 LEA RBP,[RSP-0x4F], so [RBP+0x7F] equals entry RSP+0x28, the Win64 arg5 stack slot.
resolved_sources.parent_boolean_source: 0x1401F69CB CMP [RBX+0x104], 0x1 followed by 0x1401F69DB SETZ SIL. The intervening MOVZX EAX,[RDX+0x17] does not change flags.

slot_setup.slot_cursor: 0x1401F6996 subtracts 3 from [RDX+0x10A0], then 0x1401F699D reloads it into EAX.
slot_setup.record_cursor: 0x1401F69A3 decrements [RDX+0x8DA8], then 0x1401F69AD..0x1401F69BE derives a 0x20-byte record pointer at [RDX+0x10A8 + index*0x20].
slot_setup.record_guard: 0x1401F69C1 requires [record+0x18] == 2 before the auth-code wrapper runs.
slot_setup.string_source: 0x1401F69D2 loads flags from [record+0x17]; if its high bit is set, 0x1401F69F2 dereferences [record] before copying the C string into the stack small-string at [RSP+0x30].

resolved_sources.packet_impact: For the three 0x140153190 continuation calls, selector 1 is selected when [RBX+0x104] == 1; selector 3 is selected when [RBX+0x104] != 1 and the staged +0xA0 string is six characters.

resolved_sources.wrapper_arg_source: 0x1401F68E5 MOVZX R9D, SIL; 0x140153C1B copies R9B into ESI before the wrapper's later request-prep call.
resolved_sources.parent_boolean_source: 0x1401F688B CMP [RBX+0x104], 0x1 followed by 0x1401F689B SETZ SIL. The intervening MOVZX EAX,[RDX+0x17] does not change flags.

slot_setup.slot_cursor: 0x1401F6856 subtracts 2 from [RDX+0x10A0], then 0x1401F685D reloads it into EAX.
slot_setup.record_cursor: 0x1401F6863 decrements [RDX+0x8DA8], then 0x1401F686D..0x1401F687E derives a 0x20-byte record pointer at [RDX+0x10A8 + index*0x20].
slot_setup.record_guard: 0x1401F6881 requires [record+0x18] == 2 before the auth-code wrapper runs.
slot_setup.string_source: The parent copies a C string into [RSP+0x20] and passes it to 0x140153C00 through R8.

resolved_sources.packet_impact: The 0x140153D6A path also selects selector 1 when [RBX+0x104] == 1; otherwise selector 3 when the forwarded auth string is six characters.

relationship_to_prior_table_boolean.prior_source: BPL from SETZ after comparing [R8 + RAX*4 + 0x100] with 1
relationship_to_prior_table_boolean.new_relationship: The newly resolved 0x1401F6850 and 0x1401F6990 families use the adjacent slot field [RBX+0x104] for the same selector 1 versus selector 3 decision. Together, these paths suggest the auth-code selector mode is fed from interpreter/client-script stack slot metadata or boolean slot state rather than a hard-coded UI branch.

opennxt_947_impact: OpenNXT should treat selector 1 versus selector 3 as a client-controlled auth-code mode bit that is often derived from client-script/interpreter slot state.
The same six-character auth code can travel through selector 1 or selector 3 depending on a slot boolean at +0x104. Server fixtures should not collapse the two selector forms.

The slot-parent functions also expose string extraction behavior: a record type guard [record+0x18] == 2, inline-versus-pointer handling via [record+0x17] high bit, and a small-string copy before request prep.
next_targets: Map producers of the [RBX+0x104] slot-state field so the client-script or auth UI condition that selects selector 1 can be named.
Map the structure rooted at the parent RDX argument around +0x10A0, +0x8DA8, and +0x10A8 to name the interpreter stacks involved in auth-code submission.
Correlate selector 1 and selector 3 with OpenNXT 947 auth decoder behavior and create fixtures for both forms.
Continue resolving caller-propagated +0xB8 paths at 0x140153185, 0x140153989, 0x140153AFE, 0x1401542C9, and 0x14015444A.
